falsifiable_claim: If code is open + self-hostable: infrastructure independence >= 0.7
reasoning: Sovereignty requires capability to run without external cloud dependency

falsifiable_claim: If majority requires single entity approval: sovereignty < 0.3

pattern: Epistemic independence: produce own ground truth via observation
falsifiable_claim: If beliefs rely on single external authority: epistemic_independence < 0.4
reasoning: Wisdom that depends on external validators is rented, not owned

pattern: Data sovereignty: own data, own infrastructure
falsifiable_claim: If data is on corporate platform with ToS: sovereignty < 0.6
reasoning: Platform can unilaterally change rules or delete data

pattern: Cultural preservation: defend tradition against homogenization
falsifiable_claim: If design erases local patterns for global optimization: cultural_preservation < 0.4
reasoning: Monoculture fragility. Diversity of approaches = robustness

pattern: Transparent cost: no hidden rent-seeking or subsidized dependencies
falsifiable_claim: If costs are opaque or someone subsidizes unfairly: transparency < 0.5

pattern: Capability to fork/exit: don't lock into a system
falsifiable_claim: If user cannot export data in standard format: exit_cost > 0.7
reasoning: Exit option preserves agency. Lock-in = sovereignty loss

pattern: Axiom alignment: system rules match user values explicitly
falsifiable_claim: If axioms contradict user intent: alignment < 0.3
